description: Zero-trust oriented review of Contoso's hub-and-spoke network on Azure: identity-centric access, micro-segmentation for vendor connectivity, and encrypted east-west traffic without exploding operational toil for SRE.
constraints: No flat network ACLs that require manual per-IP maintenance for SaaS egress
Regulated workloads require evidence of inspected paths for lateral movement
Maintain compatibility with existing ExpressRoute investment
requiredCapabilities: Just-in-time admin access with Privileged Identity Management patterns
Centralized flow logs and alerts without duplicating full PCAP storage
Automated posture drift detection for NSG / Azure Firewall rules
assumptions: Device compliance is already enforced for workforce identities via Conditional Access
Legacy partner VPN can be narrowed to explicit published apps over 12 months
inlineRequirements: Produce a phased kill-chain mapping (initial access → impact) for the reviewed scope

documents.name: fictional-network-context.txt
documents.content: Fictional context: two hub VNets, three landing zones, limited IPv4 overlap historically remediated.

topologyHints: Prefer Azure Firewall Manager policies for spoke inheritance where feasible
Use Private Link for PaaS data planes referenced by regulated apps
securityBaselineHints: Align to NIST 800-207 style zero-trust tenets in narrative and controls mapping
Document break-glass paths and expected monitoring for those paths
